title: OpenAI's Georgia data center pledges 'minimal' water use
excerpt: OpenAI's latest data center proposal, "Project Camellia," included pledges regarding electricity bills, water use, and Codex credits for students.
content_html:
OpenAI's latest proposal shows it's very aware that data centers are unpopular

By Natalie Musumeci

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

OpenAI is pushing to build community support for a planned new data center in Georgia in an apparent effort to avoid the type of backlash rising against AI infrastructure projects across the US.

The ChatGPT maker on Wednesday unveiled its plans to build "Project Camellia," an at least $20 billion data center campus in rural Effingham County, just outside Savannah, that will require 3.2 gigawatts of power.

OpenAI said that it has been meeting with state and local officials, community leaders, schools, and other stakeholders to gather feedback on the project and will hold a public open house on Thursday.

"We believe showing up early is the right way to build trust, with that, we are still at the beginning of the development process," OpenAI said in a press release. "Significant work remains on infrastructure, project phasing, design, financing, and operating model."

Effingham County Commissioner Jamie DeLoach said in a statement that he and his team "asked the tough questions" and found that the project "uses minimal water, won't increase energy costs for county residents, will generate new tax revenue to support our schools, and create new opportunities for local businesses."

OpenAI added it has contracted with Georgia Power to deliver electricity to the 1,400-acre campus in phases between 2028 and 2032.

OpenAI says water use will be 'minimal,' utility bills won't rise, and schools get free AI tokens

Alongside the announcement, OpenAI detailed a four-point pledge addressing issues that have fueled widespread opposition to data centers elsewhere, including electricity costs, water use, and community impacts.

OpenAI said local residents would not see higher electricity bills because of the project and that the facility's water use would be "minimal" due to its closed-loop water system.

"Georgia families will not subsidize this project. OpenAI will pay the full cost of the infrastructure and electric-service costs required to serve it," the company said.

It said that the project will be one of the first data centers designed to "proactively reduce its power consumption before residential customers are impacted during periods of high demand."

OpenAI also said it would provide $80 million in community benefits over the life of the project and will make up to $71 million in credits for Codex, its AI coding tool, available to eligible Georgia college, community college and technical school students. Georgia students who are eligible will get $100 in Codex credits through their ChatGPT account.

An independent firm will conduct an annual audit of OpenAI's commitments to be released to the public, the company said.

OpenAI expects to create thousands of construction jobs and more than 1,000 operational roles once the campus is fully built out.

Companies are racing to build AI data centers throughout the country and many communities are pushing back over concerns about the potential environmental and economic impacts.

OpenAI CEO Sam Altman acknowledged the challenge earlier this year, saying, "AI is not very popular in the US right now."

"Data centers are getting blamed for electricity price hikes. Almost every company that does layoffs is blaming AI, whether or not it really is about AI," he said.

According to the research project Data Center Watch, at least 75 data center proposals worth an estimated $130 billion were delayed or blocked during the first three months of 2026 due to local opposition.

As of May, there were at least 430 local Facebook groups with more than 525,000 members fighting back against AI data centers, according to the Data Center Opposition Report, which tracks local opposition to the projects.

Georgia's 12th Congressional District Democratic Committee has issued an "urgent call to action" via Facebook, urging community members to show up to Thursday's public meeting.

Earlier this month, New York Gov. Kathy Hochul signed an executive order imposing the nation's first statewide moratorium on large-scale data centers.

Read next

Natalie Musumeci

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Natalie is a senior reporter on Business Insider's Business News team.She was previously on BI's Legal Affairs team where she covered major cases out of state and federal court, as well as bankruptcy. Her coverage often focused on stories at the intersection of law, business, politics and technology. Natalie has covered Donald Trump’s criminal and civil cases, the wave of lawsuits against the second Trump administration, the indictment and criminal trial of Sean “Diddy” Combs, the shooting death of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, and the legal battles facing Elon Musk and his companies. Natalie came to Business Insider in June 2021 as a breaking news reporter, focusing on the most interesting angles around the trending news of the day. Natalie largely drove BI’s coverage around the fatal “Rust” shooting involving Alec Baldwin and the disappearance and murder of Gabby Petito.Prior to joining BI, Natalie worked for the New York Post, the New York Daily News, and The Brooklyn Paper. She has an extensive background covering crime and courts. During her more than 12-year journalism career, she did a stint covering the police beat out of the headquarters for the New York Police Department. Natalie, a Brooklyn native, graduated from Brooklyn College in 2012 with a journalism degree. Popular articles

  • Celebrities who partied with Diddy may want to contact their lawyers

  • At Diddy's A-list 'white parties,' naked women were a staple — but that didn't seem to raise eyebrows at the time
  • The illegal maneuvers the rich use to get richer
  • Owner of ship that crashed into Baltimore bridge will likely try to invoke 1851 law used to cap damages after Titanic disaster

blocks.html_en: OpenAI is pushing to build community support for a planned new data center in Georgia in an apparent effort to avoid the type of backlash rising against AI infrastructure projects across the US.

sentences.en: OpenAI is pushing to build community support for a planned new data center in Georgia in an apparent effort to avoid the type of backlash rising against AI infrastructure projects across the US.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 正在努力为乔治亚州计划中的新数据中心建立社区支持，这显然是为了避免美国各地针对人工智能基础设施项目的强烈反对。

blocks.html_en: The ChatGPT maker on Wednesday unveiled its plans to build "Project Camellia," an at least $20 billion data center campus in rural Effingham County, just outside Savannah, that will require 3.2 gigawatts of power.

sentences.en: The ChatGPT maker on Wednesday unveiled its plans to build "Project Camellia," an at least $20 billion data center campus in rural Effingham County, just outside Savannah, that will require 3.2 gigawatts of power.
sentences.zh: ChatGPT 制造商周三公布了其建设“Camellia 项目”的计划，这是一个耗资至少 200 亿美元的数据中心园区，位于萨凡纳郊外的埃芬汉县农村地区，需要 3.2 吉瓦的电力。

blocks.html_en: OpenAI said that it has been meeting with state and local officials, community leaders, schools, and other stakeholders to gather feedback on the project and will hold a public open house on Thursday.

sentences.en: OpenAI said that it has been meeting with state and local officials, community leaders, schools, and other stakeholders to gather feedback on the project and will hold a public open house on Thursday.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 表示，它一直在与州和地方官员、社区领袖、学校和其他利益相关者会面，收集有关该项目的反馈，并将于周四举行公众开放日。

blocks.html_en: "We believe showing up early is the right way to build trust, with that, we are still at the beginning of the development process," OpenAI said in a press release. "Significant work remains on infrastructure, project phasing, design, financing, and operating model."

sentences.en: "We believe showing up early is the right way to build trust, with that, we are still at the beginning of the development process," OpenAI said in a press release.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 在一份新闻稿中表示：“我们相信尽早出现是建立信任的正确方式，但我们仍处于开发过程的开始阶段。”

sentences.en: "Significant work remains on infrastructure, project phasing, design, financing, and operating model."
sentences.zh: “在基础设施、项目分阶段、设计、融资和运营模式方面仍有大量工作要做。”

blocks.html_en: Effingham County Commissioner Jamie DeLoach said in a statement that he and his team "asked the tough questions" and found that the project "uses minimal water, won't increase energy costs for county residents, will generate new tax revenue to support our schools, and create new opportunities for local businesses."

sentences.en: Effingham County Commissioner Jamie DeLoach said in a statement that he and his team "asked the tough questions" and found that the project "uses minimal water, won't increase energy costs for county residents, will generate new tax revenue to support our schools, and create new opportunities for local businesses."
sentences.zh: 埃芬汉县专员杰米·德洛奇在一份声明中表示，他和他的团队“提出了棘手的问题”，并发现该项目“使用最少的水，不会增加县居民的能源成本，将产生新的税收收入来支持我们的学校，并为当地企业创造新的机会。”

blocks.html_en: OpenAI added it has contracted with Georgia Power to deliver electricity to the 1,400-acre campus in phases between 2028 and 2032.

sentences.en: OpenAI added it has contracted with Georgia Power to deliver electricity to the 1,400-acre campus in phases between 2028 and 2032.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 补充说，它已与佐治亚电力公司签订合同，将在 2028 年至 2032 年间分阶段向占地 1,400 英亩的园区供电。

blocks.html_en: OpenAI says water use will be 'minimal,' utility bills won't rise, and schools get free AI tokens

sentences.en: OpenAI says water use will be 'minimal,' utility bills won't rise, and schools get free AI tokens
sentences.zh: OpenAI 表示用水量将“最小化”，水电费不会上涨，学校将获得免费的人工智能代币

blocks.html_en: Alongside the announcement, OpenAI detailed a four-point pledge addressing issues that have fueled widespread opposition to data centers elsewhere, including electricity costs, water use, and community impacts.

sentences.en: Alongside the announcement, OpenAI detailed a four-point pledge addressing issues that have fueled widespread opposition to data centers elsewhere, including electricity costs, water use, and community impacts.
sentences.zh: 除了这一声明之外，OpenAI 还详细提出了四点承诺，解决了引发人们对其他地方数据中心普遍反对的问题，包括电力成本、用水和社区影响。

blocks.html_en: OpenAI said local residents would not see higher electricity bills because of the project and that the facility's water use would be "minimal" due to its closed-loop water system.

sentences.en: OpenAI said local residents would not see higher electricity bills because of the project and that the facility's water use would be "minimal" due to its closed-loop water system.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 表示，当地居民不会因为该项目而面临更高的电费，而且由于其闭环供水系统，该设施的用水量将“最小化”。

blocks.html_en: "Georgia families will not subsidize this project. OpenAI will pay the full cost of the infrastructure and electric-service costs required to serve it," the company said.

sentences.en: "Georgia families will not subsidize this project.
sentences.zh: “乔治亚州的家庭不会资助这个项目。

sentences.en: OpenAI will pay the full cost of the infrastructure and electric-service costs required to serve it," the company said.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 将支付为其提供服务所需的全部基础设施成本和电力服务成本，”该公司表示。

blocks.html_en: It said that the project will be one of the first data centers designed to "proactively reduce its power consumption before residential customers are impacted during periods of high demand."

sentences.en: It said that the project will be one of the first data centers designed to "proactively reduce its power consumption before residential customers are impacted during periods of high demand."
sentences.zh: 该公司表示，该项目将是首批旨在“在住宅客户在高需求时期受到影响之前主动降低能耗”的数据中心之一。

blocks.html_en: OpenAI also said it would provide $80 million in community benefits over the life of the project and will make up to $71 million in credits for Codex, its AI coding tool, available to eligible Georgia college, community college and technical school students. Georgia students who are eligible will get $100 in Codex credits through their ChatGPT account.

sentences.en: OpenAI also said it would provide $80 million in community benefits over the life of the project and will make up to $71 million in credits for Codex, its AI coding tool, available to eligible Georgia college, community college and technical school students.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 还表示，它将在项目生命周期内提供 8000 万美元的社区福利，并将为其 AI 编码工具 Codex 提供高达 7100 万美元的学分，供符合条件的佐治亚大学、社区学院和技术学校的学生使用。

sentences.en: Georgia students who are eligible will get $100 in Codex credits through their ChatGPT account.
sentences.zh: 符合条件的佐治亚州学生将通过其 ChatGPT 帐户获得 100 美元的 Codex 积分。

blocks.html_en: An independent firm will conduct an annual audit of OpenAI's commitments to be released to the public, the company said.

sentences.en: An independent firm will conduct an annual audit of OpenAI's commitments to be released to the public, the company said.
sentences.zh: 该公司表示，一家独立公司将对 OpenAI 向公众发布的承诺进行年度审计。

blocks.html_en: OpenAI expects to create thousands of construction jobs and more than 1,000 operational roles once the campus is fully built out.

sentences.en: OpenAI expects to create thousands of construction jobs and more than 1,000 operational roles once the campus is fully built out.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 预计园区全面建成后将创造数千个建筑工作岗位和 1,000 多个运营岗位。

blocks.html_en: Companies are racing to build AI data centers throughout the country and many communities are pushing back over concerns about the potential environmental and economic impacts.

sentences.en: Companies are racing to build AI data centers throughout the country and many communities are pushing back over concerns about the potential environmental and economic impacts.
sentences.zh: 公司正在全国范围内竞相建设人工智能数据中心，许多社区因担心潜在的环境和经济影响而进行抵制。

blocks.html_en: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman acknowledged the challenge earlier this year, saying, "AI is not very popular in the US right now."

sentences.en: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman acknowledged the challenge earlier this year, saying, "AI is not very popular in the US right now."
sentences.zh: OpenAI 首席执行官 Sam Altman 今年早些时候承认了这一挑战，他表示：“目前人工智能在美国还不太流行。”

blocks.html_en: "Data centers are getting blamed for electricity price hikes. Almost every company that does layoffs is blaming AI, whether or not it really is about AI," he said.

sentences.en: "Data centers are getting blamed for electricity price hikes.
sentences.zh: “数据中心因电价上涨而受到指责。

sentences.en: Almost every company that does layoffs is blaming AI, whether or not it really is about AI," he said.
sentences.zh: 几乎每家进行裁员的公司都在指责人工智能，无论它是否真的与人工智能有关，”他说。

blocks.html_en: According to the research project Data Center Watch, at least 75 data center proposals worth an estimated $130 billion were delayed or blocked during the first three months of 2026 due to local opposition.

sentences.en: According to the research project Data Center Watch, at least 75 data center proposals worth an estimated $130 billion were delayed or blocked during the first three months of 2026 due to local opposition.
sentences.zh: 据研究项目“数据中心观察”称，由于当地的反对，2026 年前三个月至少有 75 个数据中心提案被推迟或阻止，价值估计为 1,300 亿美元。

blocks.html_en: As of May, there were at least 430 local Facebook groups with more than 525,000 members fighting back against AI data centers, according to the Data Center Opposition Report, which tracks local opposition to the projects.

sentences.en: As of May, there were at least 430 local Facebook groups with more than 525,000 members fighting back against AI data centers, according to the Data Center Opposition Report, which tracks local opposition to the projects.
sentences.zh: 根据跟踪当地对这些项目的反对情况的数据中心反对报告，截至 5 月，至少有 430 个当地 Facebook 群组，超过 525,000 名成员对人工智能数据中心进行反击。

blocks.html_en: Georgia's 12th Congressional District Democratic Committee has issued an "urgent call to action" via Facebook, urging community members to show up to Thursday's public meeting.

sentences.en: Georgia's 12th Congressional District Democratic Committee has issued an "urgent call to action" via Facebook, urging community members to show up to Thursday's public meeting.
sentences.zh: 佐治亚州第十二国会选区民主党委员会通过 Facebook 发出“紧急行动呼吁”，敦促社区成员出席周四的公开会议。

blocks.html_en: Earlier this month, New York Gov. Kathy Hochul signed an executive order imposing the nation's first statewide moratorium on large-scale data centers.

sentences.en: Earlier this month, New York Gov.
sentences.zh: 本月早些时候，纽约州州长。

sentences.en: Kathy Hochul signed an executive order imposing the nation's first statewide moratorium on large-scale data centers.
sentences.zh: Kathy Hochul 签署了一项行政命令，在全国范围内首次暂停大型数据中心。

sentences.zh: 娜塔莉主要推动了 BI 围绕亚历克·鲍德温 (Alec Baldwin) 致命枪击事件以及加比·佩蒂托 (Gabby Petito) 失踪和谋杀案的报道。在加入 BI 之前，娜塔莉曾在《纽约邮报》、《纽约每日新闻》和《布鲁克林报》工作。

sentences.en: She has an extensive background covering crime and courts.
sentences.zh: 她拥有犯罪和法庭方面的广泛背景。

sentences.en: During her more than 12-year journalism career, she did a stint covering the police beat out of the headquarters for the New York Police Department.
sentences.zh: 在她超过 12 年的新闻职业生涯中，她曾在纽约警察局总部报道过警察被殴打的事件。

sentences.en: Natalie, a Brooklyn native, graduated from Brooklyn College in 2012 with a journalism degree.
sentences.zh: 娜塔莉是布鲁克林人，2012 年毕业于布鲁克林学院，获得新闻学学位。

sentences.en: Georgia
sentences.zh: 乔治亚州

content_html_zh:
OpenAI的最新提案表明它非常清楚数据中心不受欢迎

作者：Natalie Musumeci 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

OpenAI 正在努力为乔治亚州计划中的新数据中心建立社区支持，这显然是为了避免美国各地针对人工智能基础设施项目的强烈反对。

ChatGPT 制造商周三公布了其建设“Camellia 项目”的计划，这是一个耗资至少 200 亿美元的数据中心园区，位于萨凡纳郊外的埃芬汉县农村地区，需要 3.2 吉瓦的电力。

OpenAI 表示，它一直在与州和地方官员、社区领袖、学校和其他利益相关者会面，收集有关该项目的反馈，并将于周四举行公众开放日。

OpenAI 在一份新闻稿中表示：“我们相信尽早出现是建立信任的正确方式，但我们仍处于开发过程的开始阶段。” “在基础设施、项目分阶段、设计、融资和运营模式方面仍有大量工作要做。”

埃芬汉县专员杰米·德洛奇在一份声明中表示，他和他的团队“提出了棘手的问题”，并发现该项目“使用最少的水，不会增加县居民的能源成本，将产生新的税收收入来支持我们的学校，并为当地企业创造新的机会。”

OpenAI 补充说，它已与佐治亚电力公司签订合同，将在 2028 年至 2032 年间分阶段向占地 1,400 英亩的园区供电。

OpenAI 表示用水量将“最小化”，水电费不会上涨，学校将获得免费的人工智能代币

除了这一声明之外，OpenAI 还详细提出了四点承诺，解决了引发人们对其他地方数据中心普遍反对的问题，包括电力成本、用水和社区影响。

OpenAI 表示，当地居民不会因为该项目而面临更高的电费，而且由于其闭环供水系统，该设施的用水量将“最小化”。

“乔治亚州的家庭不会资助这个项目。 OpenAI 将支付为其提供服务所需的全部基础设施成本和电力服务成本，”该公司表示。

该公司表示，该项目将是首批旨在“在住宅客户在高需求时期受到影响之前主动降低能耗”的数据中心之一。

OpenAI 还表示，它将在项目生命周期内提供 8000 万美元的社区福利，并将为其 AI 编码工具 Codex 提供高达 7100 万美元的学分，供符合条件的佐治亚大学、社区学院和技术学校的学生使用。 符合条件的佐治亚州学生将通过其 ChatGPT 帐户获得 100 美元的 Codex 积分。

该公司表示，一家独立公司将对 OpenAI 向公众发布的承诺进行年度审计。

OpenAI 预计园区全面建成后将创造数千个建筑工作岗位和 1,000 多个运营岗位。

公司正在全国范围内竞相建设人工智能数据中心，许多社区因担心潜在的环境和经济影响而进行抵制。

OpenAI 首席执行官 Sam Altman 今年早些时候承认了这一挑战，他表示：“目前人工智能在美国还不太流行。”

“数据中心因电价上涨而受到指责。 几乎每家进行裁员的公司都在指责人工智能，无论它是否真的与人工智能有关，”他说。

据研究项目“数据中心观察”称，由于当地的反对，2026 年前三个月至少有 75 个数据中心提案被推迟或阻止，价值估计为 1,300 亿美元。

根据跟踪当地对这些项目的反对情况的数据中心反对报告，截至 5 月，至少有 430 个当地 Facebook 群组，超过 525,000 名成员对人工智能数据中心进行反击。

佐治亚州第十二国会选区民主党委员会通过 Facebook 发出“紧急行动呼吁”，敦促社区成员出席周四的公开会议。

本月早些时候，纽约州州长。 Kathy Hochul 签署了一项行政命令，在全国范围内首次暂停大型数据中心。

阅读下一篇

Natalie Musumeci 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

Natalie 是《商业内幕》商业新闻团队的高级记者。她之前曾在 BI 法律事务团队工作，负责报道州和联邦法院以外的重大案件以及破产案件。 她的报道经常关注法律、商业、政治和技术交叉领域的故事。 娜塔莉报道过唐纳德·特朗普的刑事和民事案件、针对特朗普第二届政府的诉讼浪潮、肖恩·“老爹”库姆斯的起诉和刑事审判、联合医疗保健首席执行官布莱恩·汤普森被枪杀，以及埃隆·马斯克及其公司面临的法律斗争。 娜塔莉于 2021 年 6 月加入《商业内幕》，担任突发新闻记者，专注于当天热门新闻最有趣的角度。 娜塔莉主要推动了 BI 围绕亚历克·鲍德温 (Alec Baldwin) 致命枪击事件以及加比·佩蒂托 (Gabby Petito) 失踪和谋杀案的报道。在加入 BI 之前，娜塔莉曾在《纽约邮报》、《纽约每日新闻》和《布鲁克林报》工作。 她拥有犯罪和法庭方面的广泛背景。 在她超过 12 年的新闻职业生涯中，她曾在纽约警察局总部报道过警察被殴打的事件。 娜塔莉是布鲁克林人，2012 年毕业于布鲁克林学院，获得新闻学学位。 热门文章

  • 沃尔玛和亚马逊因使用积分系统追踪和解雇缺勤员工而面临法律麻烦：律师
  • 与吹牛老爹一起参加派对的名人可能需要联系他们的律师
  • 不受控制的人工智能可能会迎来一个新的黑暗时代
  • 在吹牛老爹的顶级“白色派对”中，裸体女性是主要内容——但这在当时似乎并没有引起人们的注意
  • 富人用来致富的非法手段
